commit.message:
chore(release): cut 0.3.0

Summary:
- bump the package version from 0.3.0-dev to 0.3.0
- move the unreleased changelog entries into the 0.3.0 section
- keep the current README messaging intact, including the disclaimer

Rationale:
- align the package metadata and release notes with the release tag
- complete the first step in the documented release checklist before publish

Validation:
- mix test
- mix docs
- mix hex.build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(architecture): sync runtime boundaries and lifecycle

Summary:
- update the supervisor map to match the current application tree
- document the current public master lifecycle and startup quiesce step
- add the simulator runtime and driver-adapter boundary to the overview

Rationale:
- keep the top-level architecture doc aligned with the actual 0.3.0 surface
- make release-facing docs consistent with the current runtime and simulator

Validation:
- not run (documentation-only change)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(readme): tighten the getting-started surface

Summary:
- add a main-branch install option alongside the latest Hex release
- refresh the quick-start driver example and lifecycle section to
  match the current public API
- call out the simulator as the fastest path when no hardware is
  available

Rationale:
- the README is the first release-facing surface and should reflect
  the current 0.3.0-dev contract accurately
- new users should have a clearer path whether they are using a real
  ring, a PREOP-first workflow, or the simulator

Validation:
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(release): clear 0.3.0 readiness blockers

Summary:
- add a small grace window for await_running/1 and
  await_operational/1 so near-boundary replies are not masked by
  local call timeouts
- block activation immediately when a slave is already carrying a
  PREOP configuration error instead of starting unrelated OP work
- update the README and unreleased changelog to match the current
  driver and simulator surface

Rationale:
- the release gate was red because startup mailbox failure paths could
  surface as await_running timeouts instead of activation failures
- release-facing docs should reflect the current 0.3.0 API and render
  correctly from the published README surface

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/integration/simulator/22_startup_mailbox_response_timeout_test.exs test/integration/simulator/21_startup_segmented_download_ack_fault_test.exs
- mix test
- mix docs
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(simulator): remove hidden-module doc type refs

Summary:
- define public simulator fault and milestone types directly on
  EtherCAT.Simulator
- drop the unused public state type alias that pointed at a hidden
  internal struct
- remove the simulator docs reference to the hidden supervisor
  module

Rationale:

  leaking private runtime implementation details

feat(dispatch): add canonical task branch lineage

Implement role-aware dispatch workspace provisioning with a deterministic canonical task branch, persisted branch-point/head metadata, and detached reviewer snapshot worktrees.

Task: @01KKFY9D7YMTSRYKY6CF77WEAN
Spec: @canonical-task-workspace-contract
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(simulator): harden faults and startup readiness

Summary:
- add builder-style simulator and UDP fault APIs with consistent queueing,
  descriptions, delayed and milestone scheduling, and richer mailbox
  protocol fault shapes
- expand simulator coverage with new mailbox, malformed CoE, delayed,
  milestone, and timeout scenarios plus updated simulator docs
- harden the simulator runtime with stricter AL validation,
  bit-accurate logical mapping, stateful DC registers, mailbox-only
  definitions, and startup bus quiescence before PREOP-ready and OP use

Rationale:
- fault injection is now easier to drive from tests and external tools,
  including `kino_ethercat`, without relying on brittle raw tuples
- the simulator catches more master regressions by modeling stricter slave
  validation and more realistic mailbox and process-image behavior
- startup/readiness now guarantees the first public PREOP mailbox exchange
  and initial OP activation run from a drained transport state instead of
  depending on harness sleeps

Validation:
- git diff --check
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: add installed/repository source flags to info and files

Extend `bpt info` and `bpt files` with `-i/--installed` and
`-r/--repository` for partid inputs. When only one flag is set, restrict
lookup to that source. When neither or both are set, search both and
prefer installed metadata or file lists over repository data if both are
available. Keep filepath and URL inputs on their existing direct code
paths so the new flags do not change those behaviors.

Update shell completions and the man page for the new options, and add
comprehensive end-to-end coverage for installed-only, repository-only,
both-source preference, and path-input behavior.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(release): 发布0.34.0并升级项目管理页面入口命令

- 新增 /打开项目管理页面 命令并兼容 /打开web 别名，统一私聊触发限制与 token+入口返回

- 同步更新智能路由白名单、命令注解、意图词表与正样本，保持 slfk 路由可识别

- 补齐命令解析与链接构造回归测试，修复私聊判定未知来源默认放行问题（fail-closed）

- 升级版本号到 0.34.0，并更新 CHANGELOG 与 README 命令说明

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: preserve deemphasized color in replace plan metadata

Upgrade and downgrade plan lines rendered the `from <pkgid>` segment with a
colored pkgid string that reset the terminal color to default. That caused
the remainder of the parenthetical metadata, including the trailing `)`, to
lose the intended deemphasized color.

Render replace metadata incrementally and reapply `Color::Deemphasize`
immediately after the colored `from` pkgid. Add a regression covering the
color reset boundary.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
version canonical filenames for bbuild files

Treat .bbuild files like .bpt files by using canonical filenames that
include both package name and version, e.g. `bpt@0.1.3.bbuild` instead
of `bpt.bbuild`.

This solves a cache invalidation bug when the bbuild version changes, as
the cache invalidation is entirely based on file path / URL.

Update the repository and test surfaces to use versioned .bbuild names
consistently for local paths, URLs, shared e2e repo setup, and fixture
files. Keep package parsing behavior unchanged while pinning the
canonical form explicitly in tests, and update build-order fixtures and
documentation to match the new naming convention.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(agent): preserve protocol context across session changes

Summary:
- restate the original task in repair and tool-result follow-up prompts
- track session continuity and clear stale session ids on provider-led session switches
- add scripted agent tests for missing session ids and explicit new-session replies

Rationale:
- IMD-7 review found that follow-up turns could lose task context when the
  provider stopped returning a reusable chat_session_id or switched sessions
- keeping the agent-side follow-up prompt self-contained fixes continuity
  without leaking provider-specific behavior into the coordinator contract

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test -p imperial-desk-agent

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

update all deps to latest

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: Bedrock mount cleanup

Previously, simultaneously attempted to remove a directory and detect if
it is non-empty (and couldn't/shouldn't be removed) in one go.  However,
did not handle EBUSY.

On Bedrock, /etc is a mount point and attempting to remove it results in
EBUSY.  Handle this explicitly.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
update all deps to latest

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: make build work with dropped privileges

When bpt processed .bbuild files as root, several issues broke the
drop-to-unprivileged-user path on Bedrock and non-Bedrock systems.

Inline sourced shell scripts instead of reopening them through
/proc/self/fd, which failed after the uid/gid switch. Route metadata
query output through a fixed single-digit fd so dash accepts the
redirection syntax. Resolve the dropped user's home from /etc/passwd,
rewrite HOME/USER/LOGNAME plus XDG/CARGO/RUSTUP env vars after the
privilege drop, and make Bedrock-local homes visible from the current
stratum by prefixing /bedrock/strata/<stratum>/ when needed. Create and
chown the effective home before dropping privileges, start metadata
queries from that home, and start builds from the intended workdir so
cross-stratum shells do not inherit an inaccessible cwd.

Stabilize manpage render tests against scdoc's generated date stamp and
add regressions for unreadable open script files, dash with double-digit
fd pressure, Bedrock path prefixing, and dropped-user environment setup.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: package missless as installable command (#28)

* feat(cli): package missless as installable command

Summary:
- bundle the CLI into a self-contained `missless` bin with a stable
  packaged schema path and a dedicated build script
- rewrite the owned docs and skill surface around the stable
  `missless` command instead of repo-relative `node .../dist/index.js`
- add packaged-install regression coverage and archive the execution
  record with review-loop, final-gate, and follow-up issue links

Rationale:
- users should be able to install the first-slice runtime locally or
  globally without depending on the current repository layout
- packaging needs tarball-level validation and workflow records so the
  stable command contract is defensible and reviewable

Tests:
- pnpm -r build
- pnpm -r typecheck
- pnpm -r test
- pnpm exec tsx --test tests/integration/cli/installable-cli.test.ts
- git diff --check
- .agents/skills/loop-final-gate/scripts/final_gate.sh .local/loop/review-20260311-142211.json .local/loop/ci-20260311-142211.json .local/loop/final-gate-20260311-142211.json

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(plan): record publish links for issue 14

Summary:
- update the completed execution record with the published PR number
- note that issue #14 was updated after publish and link the spawned
  follow-up issue in the archival record

Rationale:
- the completed plan should reflect the actual publish state instead of
  leaving stale pre-publish wording behind

Tests:
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(cli): tighten installable command validation

Summary:
- align the repo-native review wrapper and README with the stable
  `missless`-first command contract
- tighten the fake Codex integration assertions to reject repo-relative
  node entrypoint checks
- expand the packaged CLI smoke test to run installed `fetch-normalize`
  and refresh the archived execution plan with the latest review evidence

Rationale:
- the latest full-pr review found that the review-flow prompt and local
  install docs still allowed drift back to repo-relative invocation
- installed-bin coverage needed to exercise a real runtime subcommand on
  the packaged CLI surface before merge
- issue #35 captures the remaining tarball hermeticity debt without
  blocking the stable-command install contract in #14

Tests:
- pnpm exec tsx --test tests/integration/cli/e2e-driver.test.ts
- pnpm exec tsx --test tests/integration/cli/installable-cli.test.ts
- pnpm -r build
- pnpm -r typecheck
- pnpm -r test
- git diff --check
- .agents/skills/loop-review-loop/scripts/review_finalize.sh 20260312-015741 .local/loop/review-20260312-015741-*.json
- .agents/skills/loop-review-loop/scripts/review_regression.sh
- .agents/skills/loop-final-gate/scripts/stateful_gate_regression.sh

commit.message:
fix(core): harden provider SSRF and fallback policy (#25)

* fix(core): harden provider SSRF and fallback policy

Summary:
- extend fetch-normalize with shared URL safety checks for redirect hops
  and final destinations
- add explicit provider failure dispositions plus the default
  Jina Reader to direct origin fallback chain
- add direct-origin HTML normalization, targeted regressions, and the
  completed-plan/doc updates for issues #15 and #16

Rationale:
- provider-boundary SSRF policy must stay fail-closed even when redirects
  or final fetch destinations drift away from the original source URL
- fallback behavior needs an explicit contract so recoverable Jina errors
  do not weaken safety policy or hide provider-specific failures
- repository history should record the delivered slice, review outcome,
  and the remaining final-gate blocker truthfully

Tests:
- pnpm -r build
- pnpm -r typecheck
- pnpm -r test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(plan): record PR and final gate outcome

Summary:
- update the completed provider-boundary plan with PR #25 and the
  retained passing final-gate evidence
- remove the stale note about an earlier blocked gate artifact that was
  no longer retained in .local/loop

Rationale:
- the completed plan should match the current published branch state and
  remain the repository source of truth for execution history
- final-gate notes should reference only retained evidence so review and
  archival records stay auditable

Tests:
- not run (docs-only update; prior published head already passed
  `pnpm -r build`, `pnpm -r typecheck`, and `pnpm -r test`)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(core): preserve original URL in provider fetches

Summary:
- keep redirect SSRF preflight in fetch-normalize but pass the original
  source URL into provider fetches
- preserve source.json resolved_source_url from the safe preflight result
  when providers report the original input URL back
- add a regression test proving redirect preflight does not rewrite the
  provider fetch input

Rationale:
- some sources depend on redirect-chain context before the final document,
  so forcing providers to start from the terminal URL can regress fetches
- the runtime still needs the preflight-resolved destination recorded for
  auditable safety and artifact truth

Tests:
- pnpm -r build
- pnpm -r typecheck
- pnpm -r test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(plan): capture provider-slice follow-ups

* docs(plan): refresh final gate evidence

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: avoid /proc/self/fd reopen after dropping build privileges

When bpt runs as root and drops to the configured unprivileged build
user, shell helpers were still sourcing scripts via /proc/self/fd/N.
That required the child shell to reopen those paths after the uid/gid
switch, which can fail with permission denied.

Inline the already-open script contents before exec instead of relying
on /proc/self/fd for sourcing, and write query output to the inherited
pipe fd directly. Add regressions covering unreadable-but-open script
files, and remove the now-unused cloexec helper.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat!: migrate from chatgpt specific spec to offical mcp app spec (#23)

* feat(mcp-apps): migrate template to MCP Apps spec

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(deps): downgrade zod to v3 for MCP SDK compatibility

Resolves "v3Schema.safeParseAsync is not a function" error when calling tools. The MCP SDK claims to support Zod v4 but its code still uses v3 APIs like safeParseAsync() which were removed in v4.

Changes:
- Downgrade zod from ^4.2.1 to ^3.25.76 in server/package.json
- Add npm overrides in root package.json to force all dependencies to use Zod v3
- Regenerate package-lock.json with consistent Zod v3 across all packages

This is a temporary workaround until the MCP SDK is updated to properly support Zod v4.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(zod): align with MCP Apps sdk

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* feat(zod): upgrade to v4 and update EchoToolInputSchema for improved validation

* fix: got it working in VS Code

* wip

* feat(server): add UI capability negotiation and text-only fallback

Check client capabilities via getUiCapability to conditionally bind
UI resources to tools. Clients that don't advertise MCP Apps support
now receive text-only responses. Also fixes Zod error property access
(error.errors → error.issues).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(widgets): improve type safety and align with MCP Apps spec

- Add isEchoToolOutput type guard for safer structuredContent handling
- Fix requestDisplayMode return type to match spec ({ mode } object)
- Use TextContent type assertion for content array filtering
- Fix mock-app to return proper requestDisplayMode response
- Safely handle plugins array in Storybook viteFinal config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update architecture decision for McpServer + MCP Apps helpers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(server): remove non-standard CSP metadata fields

Remove scriptDomains and styleDomains from dev CSP config as they
are not part of the MCP Apps spec. Scripts, styles, fonts, and images
are all covered by resourceDomains per the McpUiResourceCsp spec.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(widgets): replace viewport with containerDimensions per MCP Apps spec

The spec defines containerDimensions (with height, maxHeight, width,
maxWidth) instead of the non-standard viewport.maxHeight field.
Updated HostContext type, Echo component, mock, and stories.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(widgets): remove jest-dom and fix mock callback reference

- Remove @testing-library/jest-dom (unnecessary with vitest)
- Replace toBeInTheDocument() with toBeTruthy()
- Add path alias to vitest config for @/ imports
- Fix mock-app spread bug that broke ontoolresult callback
  (connect() referenced the original object while the component
  set callbacks on the spread copy)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(widgets): demo openLink, sendMessage, updateModelContext and improve UX

Add new MCP Apps capability demos to the Echo widget:
- Open Docs: calls openLink() to open an external URL via the host
- Send to Chat: calls sendMessage() to send a message to the host chat
- Update Context: calls updateModelContext() to push widget state to
  the model's context, with a dedicated output section showing the
  pretty-printed JSON payload

UI improvements:
- Add icons to all action buttons (Play, X, ExternalLink, etc.)
- Move fullscreen toggle to top-right as icon-only button with
  Maximize2/Minimize2 icons that swap based on display mode
- Add title tooltips to all buttons explaining which API they call
- Fix content jump by setting min-height on result output
- Merge partial host context updates instead of replacing to fix
  fullscreen toggle requiring two clicks
- Update local display mode state from requestDisplayMode response
- Reorder buttons: Call Echo Tool, Update Context, Clear, Send to
  Chat, Open Docs

Also updates AppLike type and mock with openLink, sendMessage,
and updateModelContext methods.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(widgets): improve echo widget padding, rounded corners, and stable height

Increase default padding to 16px with a minimum floor so fullscreen mode
doesn't lose padding, add rounded-2xl corners, and reserve space for the
model context update section so the widget height stays consistent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(widgets): always enable Send to Chat button

The button sent a hardcoded demo message, not the tool output, so
gating it behind toolOutput was unnecessary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(Echo): remove min-height from main container style

* docs: document MCP Apps features added in mcp-app-compat branch

Document display modes (inline/pip/fullscreen), App API runtime methods
(openLink, sendMessage, updateModelContext, requestDisplayMode),
UI capability negotiation, inline widget assets, container dimensions,
and the createMockApp() test helper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(widgets): remove duplicate isDev declaration in vite plugin

The variable was declared twice in the same scope, causing esbuild to
fail during `vite build --watch`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* wip

* fix(dependencies): update @modelcontextprotocol/ext-apps to version 1.2.2

* feat(server): use base64 data URIs for inline widget assets (Claude.ai compat)

Claude.ai doesn't allow serving widgets from localhost like ChatGPT and
VS Code do, requiring fully self-contained HTML via INLINE_WIDGET_ASSETS.
Its sandboxed iframe on claudemcpcontent.com also uses document.write()
to inject HTML, which breaks on raw inline JS containing backticks.
Switch to base64 data URIs for JS and inline <style> tags for CSS to
work within Claude.ai's stricter CSP. Adds pre-inlining cache at startup
and file watcher for dev rebuild support.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: rename INLINE_WIDGET_ASSETS to CLAUDE_DEV_MODE

Reframe the inline widget assets env var as Claude.ai-specific local
dev mode since it's only used via `npm run dev:claude`. Document that
custom fonts won't load in Claude.ai due to its CSP restrictions
(font-src 'self' https://assets.claude.ai) and clarify this mode is
not needed in production.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add INLINE_DEV_MODE for remote sharing with inlined fonts

Add a second inline mode alongside CLAUDE_DEV_MODE:
- CLAUDE_DEV_MODE: inlines JS/CSS (fonts fall back to system stack due to CSP)
- INLINE_DEV_MODE: inlines JS/CSS + woff2 fonts as base64 data URIs

Use `npm run dev:inline` when sharing your MCP app remotely via tunnel
so viewers see custom fonts. Works with hosts like ChatGPT that don't
restrict font-src.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: document Pomerium requirement and widget hosting options

MCP server must be behind a Pomerium route for OAuth and access
policies. Widget assets must be publicly accessible via the same
server, a CDN, or a static host like Netlify/Vercel.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: document CSP resourceDomains for loading external resources in widgets

MCP Apps hosts sandbox widgets in iframes with strict CSP that blocks
remote images by default. Documenting that resourceDomains must be
declared in _meta.ui.csp to allow external image/font/API domains.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove unused build-all script

* refactor: consolidate inline dev modes into single INLINE_DEV_MODE

Remove CLAUDE_DEV_MODE in favor of a single INLINE_DEV_MODE flag that
handles both Claude.ai and remote sharing. Fonts now load via Google
Fonts instead of inlined data URIs. Also removes redundant
assetsInlineLimit default and unused build-all.mts script.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add base url to csp config

* fix: adjust resourceDomains based on INLINE_DEV_MODE

---------

feat(simulator): add startup mailbox timeout faults

Summary:
- add a mailbox-local `:drop_response` protocol fault so simulator
  devices can suppress specific CoE replies without faking transport
  loss
- add scenario 22 covering PREOP startup mailbox response timeouts for
  segmented downloads and document the new fault kind in the simulator
  docs
- update the simulator loop notes to move the next gap to public SDO
  helper timeout coverage

Rationale:
- startup mailbox failures should be able to model a slave that accepts
  a request but never produces the expected mailbox reply
- this keeps mailbox timeout behavior visible at the correct layer as a
  precise `:response_timeout`, instead of collapsing it into a generic
  bus timeout or malformed response path

Validation:
- mix test test/integration/simulator/22_startup_mailbox_response_timeout_test.exs test/integration/simulator
- mix test

commit.message:
feat(simulator): cover malformed segmented CoE responses

Summary:
- add simulator mailbox protocol faults for invalid segmented upload
  padding and unexpected segment commands
- tighten CoE segment parsing to report unexpected segment commands as a
  structured error instead of raw response blobs
- add parser-level tests and integration scenario 19 for malformed
  segmented upload responses, and move the README frontier to segmented
  download-side faults

Rationale:
- segmented mailbox regressions should surface as small parser errors the
  caller can act on, not opaque raw-frame tuples
- the simulator loop is clearer when upload-segment corruption is covered
  separately before extending into malformed download acknowledgements

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/slave/mailbox/coe_test.exs \
  test/integration/simulator/18_malformed_coe_payload_sdo_test.exs \
  test/integration/simulator/19_malformed_segment_response_sdo_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(simulator): add malformed CoE payload faults

Summary:
- add simulator mailbox protocol faults for invalid CoE payloads and
  unexpected SDO commands during upload-init handling
- add parser-level CoE coverage plus integration scenario 18 for
  malformed payloads that stay inside valid mailbox framing
- move the simulator loop docs forward to the next segmented CoE
  payload frontier

Rationale:
- mailbox regressions should surface as exact parser failures instead of
  looking like transport loss or generic mailbox faults
- the integration loop stays useful when each new parser-visible fault
  gets one focused scenario and the README advances with it

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/slave/mailbox/coe_test.exs \
  test/integration/simulator/17_malformed_mailbox_response_sdo_test.exs \
  test/integration/simulator/18_malformed_coe_payload_sdo_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(simulator): expand mailbox protocol fault coverage

Summary:
- add mailbox protocol faults for bad counters, toggle mismatches,
  wrong mailbox types, and wrong CoE services in the simulator mailbox
  runtime
- add integration scenarios 16 and 17 plus CoE parser coverage for exact
  mailbox protocol error tuples
- refresh simulator loop docs and raise the shared simulator ring test
  frame timeout to 20ms to avoid cold-start config flaps

Rationale:
- mailbox faults should stay visible as exact CoE/parser failures instead
  of looking like transport or topology loss
- the simulator integration loop is only useful if the standalone suite is
  stable enough to run repeatably, not just inside the full suite

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/slave/mailbox/coe_test.exs \
  test/integration/simulator/16_mailbox_protocol_fault_sdo_test.exs \

feat(simulator): add mailbox step milestones

Summary:
- add mailbox-step milestone tracking for successful CoE upload and
  download progress in the simulator runtime
- add a larger mailbox profile object and scenario 15 to prove delayed
  segmented aborts after real mailbox progress
- update simulator docs and scenario notes to move the next work item
  to mailbox protocol-shape faults

Rationale:
- segmented mailbox abort timing was still too coarse because faults
  could only target the next matching segment
- milestone-based mailbox progress keeps tests honest without sleeps or
  custom device behavior

feat(master): add explicit deactivation lifecycle

Summary:
- add `deactivate/0` and `deactivate(:preop)` plus a public
  `:deactivated` state
- track the desired runtime target in the master and make
  activation-blocked and recovery flows settle to OP, SAFEOP, or PREOP
- add a dedicated deactivation helper and update docs and tests for the
  new lifecycle semantics

Rationale:
- separate leaving OP from tearing the session down so callers can keep
  a live session while outputs are off
- stop reconnect and recovery paths from auto-promoting slaves back to
  OP when the runtime was intentionally deactivated

feat(simulator): add command-targeted WKC skew

Summary:
- add `{:command_wkc_offset, command_name, delta}` as a queued or sticky
  simulator fault and route it through per-datagram WKC adjustment
- add simulator visibility for active command WKC offsets and cover the new
  fault with unit tests plus integration scenario 14
- refresh simulator docs and scenario notes so the command-targeted WKC path
  is part of the public fault model and scenario loop

Rationale:
- targeted logical WKC skew covered LRW contribution faults, but the simulator
  still could not model poll-specific or command-specific WKC degradation
- health-poll failures with a healthy LRW domain are a real class of master
  recovery path and need direct regression coverage
- the simulator docs should describe the currently supported fault shapes and
  keep the scenario backlog focused on the remaining mailbox timing gap

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs test/integration/simulator/14_command_targeted_wkc_mismatch_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator --max-failures 1
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(tasks): address backfill review feedback

Add explicit own-AC coverage for @tasks-ac-backfill and keep the branch scoped to task-system changes only.

Task: @01KKB8FF9NXTCSJNYZZKHNR4KD
Spec: @tasks-ac-backfill
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(simulator): add targeted WKC and startup mailbox scenarios

Summary:
- add targeted logical WKC skew fault injection and route it only through
  logical datagram contributions
- add startup mailbox-abort coverage with a configured mailbox test driver
  plus new simulator scenarios 12 and 13
- refresh simulator docs and harden the integration harness so the standalone
  simulator suite is stable with the broader scenario set

Rationale:
- global WKC skew was too coarse to model one slave disappearing from the
  logical PDO contribution while fixed-address health polling stayed honest
- startup mailbox failures are a real PREOP activation path and needed direct
  simulator coverage, not just public SDO abort tests
- the simulator README and scenario notes should track the current fault API
  and scenario loop, and the test harness needed less aggressive defaults to
  avoid false startup flakes

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/12_startup_mailbox_abort_test.exs test/integration/simulator/13_targeted_wkc_mismatch_test.exs

- mix test

feat(simulator): add milestone-aware fault scripts

Summary:
- replace the old exchange-only script wrapper with sequential
  `{:fault_script, steps}` handling in the simulator runtime
- allow scripts to pause on `{:wait_for_milestone, ...}` and resume into
  later exchange or slave-local fault steps
- add scenario 11 and update simulator docs and existing script-based
  tests to use the new abstraction

Rationale:
- `exchange_script` stopped matching the real API once scripts needed to
  mix waits and non-exchange faults
- moving to a single sequential script runner keeps fault timing inside
  the simulator instead of pushing brittle choreography into tests
- the extra snapshot visibility makes queued and waiting script state
  observable enough for integration assertions

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs test/integration/simulator/07_combined_fault_script_test.exs test/integration/simulator/08_delayed_slave_fault_script_test.exs test/integration/simulator/09_milestone_fault_script_test.exs test/integration/simulator/11_embedded_milestone_fault_script_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator --max-failures 1
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(simulator): add milestone and segmented mailbox faults

Add milestone-triggered fault scheduling and segmented mailbox abort injection to the simulator runtime. This also fixes segmented upload response handling and adds dedicated integration coverage for milestone-aware scripts and segmented SDO aborts.

Validation: mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: align harness workflow with GitHub tooling (#807)

* feat(harness): align agent bootstrap with GitHub tooling

Summary:
- add a repo-root AGENTS.md entrypoint and remove the stale root agents.md
- replace the broken elixir bootstrap with a Python-focused worktree init flow
- move active workflow, command, and PR-body guidance to GitHub gh paths
- add regression coverage for docs sync, bootstrap dry-run, and PR body fetch

Rationale:
- origin/main still failed fresh-session bootstrap because the harness assumed an
  elixir subtree that no longer exists in this Python repository
- current unattended guidance still routed agents through stale Gitea tea paths,
  which conflicts with the available GitHub auth and the PR review feedback

Tests:
- python3 scripts/session_handover_check.py --strict
- pytest tests/test_validate_docs_sync.py tests/test_worktree_init.py tests/test_validate_pr_body.py -q
- python3 scripts/validate_docs_sync.py
- bash .codex/worktree_init.sh --dry-run
- bash .codex/worktree_init.sh
- gh auth status && git ls-remote origin HEAD && gh pr status
- ruff check scripts tests

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(ci): tolerate missing rg in backtest gate

Summary:
- send backtest gate log output to stderr so auto mode selection stays clean
- fall back to grep when rg is unavailable in the runner environment
- add a regression test for auto mode without rg on PATH

Rationale:
- PR #807 surfaced a real CI-only failure where the backtest gate crashed
  before deciding skip/full because the hosted runner lacked rg
- the resulting log text leaked into selected_mode and produced an invalid
  BACKTEST_MODE failure even for docs-only changes

Tests:
- pytest tests/test_backtest_gate.py tests/test_validate_docs_sync.py tests/test_worktree_init.py tests/test_validate_pr_body.py -q
- BACKTEST_MODE=auto BASE_REF=origin/main FORCE_FULL_BACKTEST=false bash scripts/backtest_gate.sh
- ruff check scripts tests

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(ci): align governance validator with github harness

Summary:
- switch governance asset validation to .github PR template requirements
- require gh-based workflow and command tokens instead of tea_comment markers
- keep regression coverage green alongside the backtest-gate and harness tests

Rationale:
- PR #807 test workflow still enforced stale Gitea governance tokens after the
  docs were correctly moved to the GitHub harness path
- the validator should guard the active unattended workflow, not force the docs
  back toward deprecated tea-based instructions

Tests:
- pytest tests/test_validate_governance_assets.py tests/test_backtest_gate.py tests/test_validate_docs_sync.py tests/test_worktree_init.py tests/test_validate_pr_body.py -q
- python3 scripts/validate_governance_assets.py 92b783f078c159dc224b42d687cdaa2d84aca9e4...HEAD
- ruff check scripts tests

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix task dispatch and todolist visibility

Repair Task Hub claim flow so eligible open tasks are actually claimed by heartbeat, stop queue rebuild from touching updated_at, and add completed/history APIs with session/run-log links.

Update To Do List UI with completed jobs panel, task history review flow, and force-next-heartbeat controls. Add regression tests for dispatch claiming, duplicate system-command handling, and new dashboard endpoints.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(runtime): add delayed simulator faults and limits

Summary:
- add delayed simulator fault scheduling with `{:after_ms, delay_ms,
  fault}` and expose `scheduled_faults` in simulator snapshots
- add the new `08` delayed slave-fault integration scenario and update
  simulator docs to reflect the expanded fault API
- reject impossible master topology/address combinations and stabilize
  the PREOP mailbox abort scenario with a less brittle timeout budget

Rationale:
- mixed recovery scenarios need delayed slave-local mutations without
  relying on brittle sleeps or oversized exchange scripts
- address-space limits should fail explicitly before the runtime tries
  to encode invalid auto-increment or station addresses
- the mailbox abort test is about CoE abort behavior, not transport
  timeouts during its initial PREOP roundtrip

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/08_delayed_slave_fault_script_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(conductor): rename connector tool directory to conductor

Summary:
- Renamed the connectors folder to conductor.
- Updated the symphonyctl registry path and related tests/import paths to
  use conductor.
- Kept behavior unchanged; only path/layout references were migrated.

Rationale:
- Aligns codebase naming with the current product direction (conductor).
- Prevents future confusion between old connector naming and the actual file
  layout.

Tests:
- Not run (not requested).

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
